title: SetError lacks Display impl
description: SetError is the per-object failure type returned in SetResponse.not_created / not_updated / not_destroyed. Despite being named 'error' and representing operation failures, it only derives Debug and Deserialize with no Display impl. Callers who want to surface set errors in UI or logs must manually format: format\!("{}: {}", err.error_type, err.description.unwrap_or_default()). Fix: add impl Display for SetError that formats as "{error_type}: {description}" (using description or "(no description)" when None). Optionally implement std::error::Error.
